title: rubus exaltatus var. fastigiatus
intro: Rubus exaltatus var. fastigiatus, also known as the Himalayan raspberry, is a variety of the Rubus exaltatus plant, a type of bramble that belongs to the rose family. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties, which are derived from its leaves, stems, and roots. The Himalayan raspberry has been associated with several health benefits, including reducing blood sugar levels, improving cardiovascular health, and possessing anti-cancer properties. The plant can be prepared in various herbal forms, including teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to treat various ailments, including digestive issues and skin problems.
